---
title: BannerPlugin
description: Add a banner to the top or bottom of each generated chunk
---

Adds a banner (comment) to the top or bottom of each generated chunk.

## Usage

```js
const webpack = require('webpack');

module.exports = {
  plugins: [
    new webpack.BannerPlugin({
      banner: '/*! Copyright (c) 2024 MyCompany */'
    })
  ]
};
```

## Constructor

The plugin accepts either a string, function, or options object:

```js
// String format
new webpack.BannerPlugin('banner text');

// Function format
new webpack.BannerPlugin(() => {
  return `Build: ${new Date().toLocaleString()}`;
});

// Options object
new webpack.BannerPlugin({
  banner: 'banner text',
  raw: false,
  entryOnly: false
});
```

## Options

<ParamField path="banner" type="string | function" required>
  The banner text to add. Can be a string or a function that returns a string.

  When using a function, it receives an object with the following properties:
  - `hash` - The compilation hash
  - `chunk` - The chunk object
  - `filename` - The filename being processed

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: (data) => {
      return `File: ${data.filename}\nChunk: ${data.chunk.name}`;
    }
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="raw" type="boolean" default={false}>
  If `true`, the banner will not be wrapped in a comment.

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: '#!/usr/bin/env node',
    raw: true
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="entryOnly" type="boolean" default={false}>
  If `true`, the banner will only be added to entry chunks.

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: 'This is an entry chunk',
    entryOnly: true
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="test" type="string | RegExp | Array<string | RegExp>">
  Include all modules that pass test assertion.

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: 'JS files only',
    test: /\.js$/
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="include" type="string | RegExp | Array<string | RegExp>">
  Include all modules matching any of these conditions.

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: 'Source files',
    include: /\/src\//
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="exclude" type="string | RegExp | Array<string | RegExp>">
  Exclude all modules matching any of these conditions.

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: 'No vendor files',
    exclude: /node_modules/
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="footer" type="boolean" default={false}>
  If `true`, the banner will be placed at the end of the output instead of the beginning.

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: '/* End of file */',
    footer: true
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="stage" type="number">
  The compilation stage at which the banner should be added. Uses webpack's `PROCESS_ASSETS_STAGE_*` constants.

  ```js
  new webpack.BannerPlugin({
    banner: 'Custom timing',
    stage: webpack.Compilation.PROCESS_ASSETS_STAGE_ADDITIONS
  });
  ```
</ParamField>

## Examples

### Add Copyright Notice

```js
new webpack.BannerPlugin({
  banner: '/*! Copyright (c) 2024 MyCompany. All rights reserved. */'
});
```

### Add Shebang for CLI Tools

```js
new webpack.BannerPlugin({
  banner: '#!/usr/bin/env node',
  raw: true,
  entryOnly: true,
  test: /\.js$/
});
```

### Dynamic Build Information

```js
new webpack.BannerPlugin({
  banner: () => {
    return [
      '/**',
      ` * Build date: ${new Date().toISOString()}`,
      ` * Version: ${process.env.npm_package_version}`,
      ' */',
    ].join('\n');
  }
});
```

### Add Source Map Pragma

```js
new webpack.BannerPlugin({
  banner: '//# sourceMappingURL=[url]',
  raw: true,
  entryOnly: false,
  test: /\.js$/
});
```
